edus.text: The United States and five other world powers have reached a deal with Iran
that would place strict limits on Tehran ' s nuclear program in return for ending sanctions on its economy ,
the culmination of years of delicate diplomacy
pursued by President Barack Obama
despite warnings
the agreement could strengthen Iran ' s Islamist regime
and leave it dangerously close to a nuclear bomb .
The historic accord ,
reached by Secretary of State John Kerry and his international counterparts in Vienna on Tuesday after 18 days of intense negotiations ,
now faces review from a hostile Republican-led Congress , opposition from every GOP presidential candidate , from Israel ' s government and from Sunni Arab monarchs .
The deal ' s long and complex implementation process also leaves it vulnerable to unraveling .
Speak
ng from the White House Tuesday morning ,
bama called the deal a victory for diplomacy
hat would prevent a nuclear arms race in the Middle East
nd avert a possible conflict with Iran .
No deal means a greater chance of more war in the Middle East , "
bama said .
e reaffirmed America ' s commitment to Israel ' s security and Gulf Arab states like Saudi Arabia ,
hile adding
hat the U.S. is " open to engagement on the basis of mutual interests and mutual respect . "
bama also hinted at the possibility of a larger thaw in U.S.-Iranian relations .
It is possible to change , "
bama told Iranians ,
rging them to take a " different path , one of tolerance , of peaceful resolution to conflict...
his deal opens an opportunity
o move in a new direction .
e should seize it . "
This is the good deal
hat we have sought , "
erry said in a statement from Vienna .
f it succeeds ,
he agreement could upgrade Obama ' s checkered foreign policy legacy ,
s well provide a crowning achievement for Kerry ' s 30-year political career .

nalysts call it one of the modern era ' s most important arms control agreements , in a league with the 1970 international Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty and the 1994 START nuclear missile treaty between the U.S. and the Soviet Union .
ome believe
t could begin to normalize relations between Iran and the West after three decades of cold war ,
lthough Obama and Kerry have denied
hat is their goal ,
hile others predict
hat an emboldened Iran will seek greater dominance across the Middle East .
ut on Tuesday the European Union ' s top foreign policy official , Frederica Mogherini , called the pact " much more than a nuclear deal , "
dding :
It can open a new chapter . "
n Twitter , Iran ' s president , Hassan Rouhani said
hat with the " unnecessary crisis resolved ,
ew horizons emerge with a focus on shared challenges . "
hat will hardly please Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ,
ho tweeted :
From the initial reports we can already conclude
hat this agreement is a historic mistake . "
ccording to a 159-page document
osted online by Russia ' s foreign ministry ,
hich it called the final text of the deal ,
U.N. resolution
ifting sanctions
ill also express the Security Council ' s " desire
o build a new relationship with Iran . "
he text says also
hat Iran has vowed
hat " under no circumstances " will it ever " seek , develop or acquire any nuclear weapons. "
nd it describes a joint commission
omposed of its seven parties
o monitor the deal ' s implementation .
ne issue
hat will draw close scrutiny from nuclear experts
s Iran ' s ability
o research advanced nuclear technologies
hile its program is constrained by a deal .
ran now relies on 1970s-era centrifuges
o enrich uranium ,
hich are highly inefficient .
he document
osted by Russian foreign ministry
ays
hat after 81⁄2 years Iran can begin testing up to 30 modern IR-6 and IR-8 centrifuges .
nalysts fear
hat ,
f Iran masters top-quality centrifuges ,
t might be capable of producing a bomb in a matter of months or weeks soon after the deal ' s main restrictions lift in a decade .
n an issue
hat snagged the talks in their final days ,
he deal will free Iran from an arms embargo after five years ,
bama said Tuesday .
bama has made the deal a central plank of his foreign policy .
ating from early in the 2008 presidential campaign ,
e called for fresh thinking toward American adversaries like Iran .
s president , he has argued
hat it is wiser to negotiate with a nemesis of more than three decades
han risk a military confrontation over Iran ' s nuclear ambitions .
ut he has also insisted ,
n response to the many critics
ho called him desperate for a deal ,
hat signing off on a weak agreement is not in his interest .
If Iran has a nuclear weapon ,
t ' s my name on this , "
he president said in May
a measure
f just how keenly aware he is of the deal ' s implications for his legacy .
he deal came
8 days after Kerry arrived in Vienna for a round of talks
hose initial deadline was June 30 .
s early July came and went ,
he talks bogged down in a handful of issues ,
ncluding whether and how to lift a U.N. embargo on Iran ' s import and export of conventional arms .
erry , too , was accused of wanting a deal too badly , a charge
is marathon session in Vienna
all of it on crutches ,
s he recovers from a broken leg -
ay have defused .
he comprehensive deal fills in the details of a preliminary framework deal
eached in April by the U.S. and its negotiating partners
France , Germany , Britain , China and Russia .
document
aid to be about 100 pages
ill be transmitted to Congress
nd made public ,
lthough some portions are expected to remain classified .
bama ' s main goal has been to impose limits on Iran ' s fast-advancing nuclear program
o ensure
hat Tehran can ' t acquire a nuclear bomb faster
han the U.S.
nd its allies can act
o stop it .
he deal seeks to do that
y reducing Iran ' s capacity
o produce highly enriched uranium and plutonium ,
lowing its research and development into nuclear technology ,
nd imposing inspections and monitoring of Iran ' s nuclear activities
o prevent cheating .
he goal is to achieve a " breakout time "
or the time
t would take Iran
o produce the nuclear material
equired for one bomb -
f at least one year .
hat is long enough ,
.S. officials believe ,
or the world community to take punitive action
hat could include airstrikes against Iranian facilities .
ome elements of the deal were locked in by an April 2 political framework agreement
eached in Lausanne , Switzerland .
hey include a commitment by Iran to reduce its number of installed centrifuges from 19,000 to 6,104 ,
ith only 5,060 of those enriching uranium for 10 years .
entrifuges spin gaseous uranium at supersonic speeds
o increase its purity to levels suitable for a nuclear weapon .
ran has also agreed to modify a plutonium-fueled nuclear reactor
o that its fuel can not be reprocessed for use in a weapon .
nd it will allow inspectors from the International Atomic Energy Agency broad access to suspected nuclear sites , as well as cooperate with an IAEA investigation into its past activities ,
lthough many crucial details have yet to be released .
he comprehensive agreement will leave many powerful critics deeply dissatisfied .
etanyahu ,
ho sees Iran as an existential threat to his country ,
as said repeatedly
hat the deal will " pave Iran ' s path to the bomb " ;
everal Sunni Arab states ,
specially Saudi Arabia ,
orry
hat the end of sanctions will free up tens of billions of dollars
hat Iran can direct to militant allies like Hezbollah and sectarian battles from Iraq to Syria to Yemen .
he emerging outlines of the deal have also drawn withering fire for months from Capitol Hill ,
ueled by Republicans
and many Democrats -
ho agree with Netanyahu
hat it jeopardizes Israel ' s security .
vote of disapproval by Congress would prevent Obama from lifting sanctions on Tehran ,
lthough administration officials believe
hey can maintain enough Democratic backing
o sustain a presidential veto .
he nuclear talks began after years of Western worry over Iran ' s nuclear aims ,
espite Tehran ' s insistence
hat its program was only for scientific and energy purposes .
he 2002 revelation of Iran ' s uranium enrichment plant at Natanz and a plutonium reactor at Arak
sites previously undeclared to the U.N. in violation of Iran ' s international commitments -
adly undercut Tehran ' s claims .
o did the 2009 discovery of a secret , underground enrichment facility
uried in a mountain at Fordow , near the holy city of Qom .
n 2007 , an assessment by the U.S. intelligence community found
hat Iran had pursued a military dimension to its nuclear program
in effect , an Iranian Manhattan Project -
ut ceased the work in 2003 .
ran has failed to comply fully with IAEA inspectors
ursuing the question , a major point of contention in the talks .
he United States ,
hich broke off diplomatic relations with Iran after the country ' s 1979 Islamic revolution and capture of the U.S. Embassy in Tehran ,
as long used sanctions and other tools
o punish Iran for its behavior .
n recent years the sanctions vice tightened considerably
s Russia and China backed harsh U.N. measures ,
he U.S. Congress targeted Iran ' s financial sector ,
nd the Obama administration persuaded growing economies like India and South Korea agreed to sharply limit their consumption of Iranian oil .
t the same time ,
fter taking office
bama continued a Bush-era program
argeting Iran ' s nuclear infrastructure with cyberwar , in the form of the Stuxnet virus ,
hich delayed Iran ' s enrichment program .
he U.S. , U.N. and EU sanctions badly hurt Iran ' s economy ;
nflation soared above 40 percent in 2013 .
.S. sanctions
hreatening to punish foreign financial institutions
or doing business with Iran
anded a particularly painful blow .
n mid-2012 ,
fter exchanging messages through the Arab state of Oman ,
ranian and American officials secretly met
o lay the groundwork for potential nuclear talks .
his involved diplomats
ispatched by then-Secretary of State Hillary Clinton ,
ncluding her close aide Jake Sullivan , now a top adviser to her presidential campaign .
alks became possible in June 2013 ,
fter Iranians elected as president a relatively moderate Iranian cleric , Hassan Rouhani .
e offered the West a more conciliatory face than his hardline predecessor , Mahmoud Ahmadinejad .
ith the apparent blessing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ,
ho has the final word on all state decisions in Iran ,
ouhani spoke directly via phone with Obama in September 2013 ,
icking off the public phase of the nuclear talks .
espite a willingness
o bargain over its nuclear program ,
ran has pursued an aggressive foreign policy elsewhere in the Middle East ,
larming its neighbors .
or example , Iran is backing Syrian President Bashar Assad against Sunni groups
rying to topple him .
t the same time , Iranian-trained Shiite militias are indirectly helping U.S.-backed Iraqi troops
rying to defeat the Islamic State terrorist group in Iraq .
nd in Yemen , Iran has backed the Houthi rebels ,
hose positions are being bombed by Saudi Arabia with U.S. assistance .
ritics of the deal fear
hat
nce Iran starts to get relief from sanctions
including access to more than $ 100 billion in frozen assets -
t will use the money
o wreak more havoc in the region .
.S. officials argue
hat Iran ' s regional meddling costs little ,
nd that the country is more likely to direct the influx of funds to repairing its economy and its infrastructure .
t the same time , Obama has promised more military aid to Arab countries
nd repeatedly reaffirmed his commitment to Israel ' s security .
n many ways , the hardest part of the nuclear deal begins now , with its implementation .
ongress will review the deal ,
nd is likely to vote
o prevent Obama from suspending sanctions on Iran
a measure
bama is sure to veto .
n that scenario , the votes of a few dozen Senate Democrats could become decisive in a veto override battle .
he nuclear deal will also drive the 2016 presidential campaign ' s foreign policy debate .
everal Republican contenders are already vowing to kill the deal
f they win the White House .
